title: Add first-season event pool
summary: Add the bounded M5 first-season event pool with cooldowns, recovery opportunities, anomaly/faction/governance hooks and legal command scheduling.
acceptance: M5SeasonEventPoolStore owns active event pool rows cooldowns recovery windows event freshness precondition failures and pool version
Event candidates include anomaly faction governance season resource and recovery basis versions and are selected through bounded stable Top-K lanes
The director schedules legal commands or opportunities only and cannot directly mutate source owner facts
Season pool includes resource pressure registration pressure market night bridge-route pressure archive damage risk and recovery opportunities
Focused tests cover event preconditions cooldowns recovery-window suppression wrong-type recovery rejection structured reasons queue bounds and deterministic random stream use

history.details: Created by WM-0072 M5 planning DAG; remains proposed until dependencies are done.

objective: Implement the first deterministic season/event pool needed by M5 alpha content without creating an unbounded quest generator.
forbiddenPaths: direct director mutation of owner facts
risksAndFileOwnership: Risk: event pool becomes a hidden director brain; it must schedule legal commands only.
Risk: cooldown/recovery queues grow without bound; tests and metrics must assert bounded lanes.
Risk: season events bypass faction/governance basis; candidates must carry source versions.
benchmarkImpact: Adds season event candidate cooldown recovery precondition and queue metrics; baseline update waits for WM-0083.
reviewRouting: Simulation-engineer completes to reviewer; systems-architect must be consulted before changing director authority or public schema.
rollbackModel: Revert M5 season/event pool, tests, content schema hooks and docs; keep anomaly and faction/governance stores intact.
ownerGate: Use bounded event lanes cooldowns and recovery windows
Block on public protocol save schema dependency UI authority or M6 scope

sparkEligibility.reason: Season/event director integration touches simulation authority and deterministic scheduling.
